title: Speedboat Rental Hourly in Marbella Port: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Marbella Port offers some of the Costa del Sol's best opportunities for a speedboat rental hourly, with prices starting at around €180 per hour for a high-performance vessel. BoatHire24 connects you with trusted local boat owners for a seamless booking experience.

What does it cost to rent a speedboat hourly in Marbella Port?

Hourly rental rates for a speedboat in Marbella Port range from €150 to €350, depending on the boat size and season. For a standard 28-foot motorboat you can expect to pay around €200 per hour in the high season. Many owners include fuel and basic insurance in the hourly rate, but it is wise to confirm before booking. You can browse boats for your dates directly on the platform and compare verified boat listings at browse boats for your dates.

Where to set off in Marbella Port

The main departure point is Puerto Deportivo de Marbella, a modern marina in the town centre. Nearby, Marina Puente Romano offers an alternative starting point with easy access to the open sea. From either marina, you can head west towards Cabopino Harbour or east to the glamorous Puerto Banús. Many skippers also recommend a run to the island of Isla de las Palomas or a stop at the beaches of San Pedro Alcántara. The distance from Marbella Port to Puerto Banús is about 4 nautical miles, a short but scenic journey along the coast.

Which boat to rent for an hourly charter in Marbella Port?

  1. Sports speedboat (e.g. Axopar 28), ideal for a two-hour blast along the coast; carries up to 8 guests; from €200 per hour.
  2. Day cruiser (e.g. Fairline 38), perfect for a three-hour family trip; sleeps 6 comfortably; from €250 per hour.
  3. Rib with outboard, great for water sports and agility; takes 6 passengers; from €150 per hour.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 50), premium experience for up to 12 guests; from €350 per hour.

For a premium yet agile option, this speedboat provides a proven choice with excellent handling and modern amenities.

Seasons and licence requirements

High season runs from June to September, when the levante wind can create lively sea conditions. The rest of the year offers quieter sailing with reduced rates. Regarding licences, for a speedboat under a certain power threshold, no licence is necessary in Spain, but the operator will confirm exact requirements at booking. For larger or more powerful vessels, a Spanish recreational boating licence or an international equivalent is mandatory. The operator always provides a full briefing and safety induction.

Local tip: In Marbella Port, book your morning slot (9am, 12pm) to enjoy calm waters and avoid afternoon winds. Afternoon chubascos are rare but can pick up quickly.

At a glance

  • Price range: €150, €350 per hour
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 1, 4 hours typical
  • Capacity: 6, 12 guests on most boats
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella Port

Can I rent a speedboat hourly without a licence in Marbella Port?

Many speedboats under 15hp (11kW) can be hired without a licence, but you should confirm with the owner as rules vary.

What is the best time of day for an hourly rental?

Morning departures between 9am and 11am offer the calmest sea conditions and the best visibility.

Do I need to pay a deposit for an hourly speedboat rental?

A refundable security deposit of €500 to €1,000 is typically required, depending on the boat's value.

Are fuel costs included in the hourly price?

Most listings include fuel for a booked route, but always check the description or ask the owner before setting off.
